etymology.description: The word 竹果 (zhúguǒ) combines 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") and 果(果) (guǒ, "fruit").
etymology.interpretation: The combination suggests a fruit that is associated with bamboo, possibly referring to the fruit produced by a bamboo plant or a fruit that is commonly found in bamboo forests. In traditional Chinese culture, bamboo is often seen as a symbol of resilience and strength.
